evals.name: vague-prompt-asks-before-rewriting
evals.prompt: Improve this prompt for Claude Code: "fix the login bug"
evals.expected_output: Should first fetch https://code.claude.com/docs/en/best-practices, then recognize the prompt is missing symptom, location, and definition of fixed, and ask 1-3 targeted questions (what do users observe, where does auth live, how to verify the fix) BEFORE rewriting. Should NOT attempt to fix any bug itself and should NOT invent requirements.

evals.name: clear-prompt-one-shot-rewrite
evals.prompt: Rewrite my prompt: "add tests for src/utils/date.ts — we use Vitest, I care about DST edge cases, done means npm test passes, don't touch the implementation"
evals.expected_output: Should fetch the best-practices page, then rewrite in ONE shot (no clarifying questions — scope, verification, and non-goals are all present). Output: the rewritten prompt in a fenced code block (scoped task, verification command with a demand for evidence, explicit do-not-touch), a bulleted what-changed-and-why list tied to best-practice rules, and an offer to run it now or paste into a fresh session.

evals.name: fetch-failure-uses-fallback
evals.prompt: (WebFetch is unavailable in this environment.) Make this prompt better: "refactor the payment service, it's getting messy"
evals.expected_output: Should attempt the live fetch, fail, then fall back to references/best-practices-checklist.md and explicitly warn the user the guidance may be stale. Should still proceed: the prompt is ambiguous (which files, what does better look like, what must not break), so it asks targeted questions before rewriting.

evals.name: negative-trigger-llm-app-system-prompt
evals.prompt: Help me write a good system prompt for my customer-support chatbot built on the Claude API
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger this skill — the request is prompt engineering for the user's own LLM application, not a prompt aimed at Claude Code. The Claude API / claude-api guidance applies instead.
